findings.description: Alignment of the memo header block is lost.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the contents of the 'To', 'From', and 'Subject' fields are vertically aligned to form a distinct column. In the HTML, they are rendered as standard left-aligned text.
The Roman numeral list (I, II, III, IV) in the PDF is indented from the left margin. In the HTML, it is left-aligned with the body text.

findings.description: Underline formatting is lost on list item lead-in text.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the lead-in phrases for bulleted items (e.g., 'Beneficial Reuse Areas') are both bold and underlined. In the HTML, they are only bolded.

findings.description: Underline formatting is lost on list item lead-in text.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the lead-in phrases for bulleted items (e.g., 'Environmental Justice') are both bold and underlined. In the HTML, they are only bolded.
